searchUrlAttributes.meta_description: Here are the top BMW 3 Series listings for sale ASAP. Check the carfax, find a low miles 3 Series, view 3 Series photos and interior/exterior features. Search by price, view certified pre-owned 3 Seriess, filter by color and much more. What will be your next ride?
searchUrlAttributes.title: 50 Best Used BMW 3 Series for Sale, Savings from $1,769
searchUrlAttributes.meta_description_es: Aquí están los mejores listados de BMW 3 Series a la venta lo antes posible. Verifique el carfax, busque millas bajas en 3 Series, vea las fotos de 3 Series y las características interiores / exteriores. Busque por precio, vea 3 Seriess usados ​​certificados, filtre por color y mucho más. ¿Cuál será tu próximo vehículo?
searchUrlAttributes.title_es: Los 50 mejores BMW 3 Series usados en venta, ahorros desde $1,769

ownerReviews.title: Love my BMW
ownerReviews.body: I own a 2001 BMW 325i. I love this vehicle. Yes, it's older, but it still looks very modern. The body design is still beautiful and people can appreciate that. It is very well made, easy to work on for at-home mechanics and the ride is smooth.
ownerReviews.pros: This car got excellent crash ratings, so it would be very safe in an accident. It has front and side airbags and anti-lock brakes. The doors lock automatically when you start to drive. The ride is very comfortable despite needing some suspension components replaced. It stops on a dime even though the brakes need to be redone. The doors are light and easy to open. There is a ton of space in the trunk, enough for 4 big suitcases plus some. The car is beautifully designed and very classy looking.
ownerReviews.cons: There are hardly any negatives with this car. The leather on the door around the airbag is peeling but it is a quick fix with gorilla glue, no need to remove the door or door panel. The sunroof motor cover is difficult to attach, poorly designed. The cupholders need to be bigger and are a little awkward to use under the armrest. The plastic compartments on the dash are becoming sticky and gummed up due to heat and age.
ownerReviews.story: Just today I stalled out 3 times driving my son to school. It was very busy, lots of cars, so this was inconvenient. For the manual transmission, you have to hold the clutch in while driving in 1st gear otherwise it stalls.

ownerReviews.title: Great introduction into luxury vehicles.
ownerReviews.body: The BMW 328i is a very well-rounded vehicle. It is luxurious but accessible to young professionals who are still saving and earning their way in the job market. It has excellent performance, great features and is very easy on the eyes. The new car's electrically assisted power steering is quite good (and, thanks to software updates, is supposed to get better over time). It is also very good value, when comparing it to other luxury models. A few negatives include gas mileage and cost of ownership. Gas mileage is not the best - although they advertise an estimated 32 MPG city, the car actually gets closer to 20. Another negative is the cost of repair. If you choose to own this vehicle long-term, you can expect to pay significantly for major repairs. Another slightly less important negative is that in order to cut costs, the company seems to cut a few corners. For example, LED lights are not standard (is becoming the norm even in economy brands nowadays), neither is GPS/navigation. Adding these features from the manufacturer costs significantly (at least 5,000 more for LED lights and $2,000 for the GPS/navigation system). Overall, though it is an excellent choice.
ownerReviews.pros: The performance is spectacular. I like the smart suspension and active steering features the most.
ownerReviews.cons: Gas mileage and cost of ownership (insurance, repairs, payments); some corner-skipping, in terms of options.
ownerReviews.story: Driving in the mountains on the way to a camping trip really showcased the performance and engineering that gives this brand and model it's stellar reputation. Sometimes these things are hard to notice on the regular city commute.

ownerReviews.title: I love 325i.
ownerReviews.body: When I first test drove my car I fell in love and paid cash for it on the spot. I test drove it in the rain and it handled like a champ. Heated seats make me appreciate it more during the winter. Most problems are cosmetic and are more likely the result of old age/wear & tear versus, and not manufacturer defects. For example some of the silver trim on the inside of the car is starting to come loose, as is the upholstery. At this point the radio is a bit dated as it has a CD player but no auxiliary input. But in terms of drivability and sleekness, I'm very satisfied. My kids love it and have sense of pride jumping out of the nice car, older though it may be. My six year old for example loves sitting up front, especially when we go to a drive through or a car wash. Prior to the older model BMW I drove a newer model American are, and it wouldn't have even come close to competing. The BMW has great acceleration and when I need to low down suddenly, it stops on a dime. All in all, I'm very content with my decision to purchase this vehicle, though I initially had some hesitation about going from a newer model to an older model car.
ownerReviews.cons: Cosmetic flaws: interior trim, upholstery, etc.
ownerReviews.story: I got it up to 110 mph once :).

articlePreviews.contentSummary: See all the BMW 3 Series generations here. The BMW 3 Series has comprised of seven generations so far.
articlePreviews.title: BMW 3 Series Generations

articlePreviews.contentSummary: BMW’s venerable 3 Series sedan gets a healthy update for the 2021 model year. But does it still retain the characteristics that have made the nameplate the benchmark for sport sedans? Read our review to find out.
articlePreviews.title: 2021 BMW 3 Series Review

articlePreviews.contentSummary: BMW’s all-new, seventh-generation 3 Series is on its way with more power, more space and more tech goodies. But can the new 330i and M340i deliver on the promises made by their deft-handling predecessors? Our testing says yes.
articlePreviews.title: Driven: 2019 BMW 330i and 2020 M340i
